criteria_results.description: Pressing Ctrl+C when an object is selected updates the clipboardObject in DocumentState.
criteria_results.evidence: Implemented in client/src/lib/editor-context.tsx (lines 183-188) using 'clipboardObjects' (plural) to support multi-object copy, and triggered in client/src/components/editor/Canvas.tsx (lines 68-71).

criteria_results.description: Pressing Ctrl+V creates a new object in the objects array with a unique UUID.
criteria_results.evidence: Implemented in client/src/lib/editor-context.tsx (line 195) within the PASTE_OBJECT case, using uuidv4() for each duplicated object.

criteria_results.description: Pasted objects are placed with a coordinate offset relative to the original.
criteria_results.evidence: Implemented in client/src/lib/editor-context.tsx (lines 193-197), applying an offset of 20 / state.scale to x and y coordinates.

criteria_results.description: Keyboard listeners are cleaned up on component unmount.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/components/editor/Canvas.tsx (line 80), the useEffect hook returns window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown).

criteria_results.description: Paste operation is blocked if the clipboard is empty or no layer is active.
criteria_results.evidence: In client/src/lib/editor-context.tsx (line 191), the PASTE_OBJECT case includes a guard: if (state.clipboardObjects.length === 0 || !state.activeLayerId) return state;

suggestions: The implementation correctly evolved the requirement to support multiple selected objects (clipboardObjects: EditorObject[]) instead of just one, which is a better user experience.
The addition of updating the clipboard with the newly pasted objects in the PASTE_OBJECT reducer allows for sequential pasting with increasing offsets, which is very helpful for users.
